D2 Vision L2 Understand Reference

Three Channels of Colour

slide three primaries and watch every colour collapse into a single L, M, S triple

Because the eye carries only three cone types, any mixture of light is encoded as exactly three numbers — the L, M, and S responses.

Slide the red, green, and blue primary intensities. Watch the mixed colour above and the L, M, S bars below — countless spectra map onto the same three cone numbers, which is why three primaries suffice.
